query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "reproducing, analyzing, and detecting reward hacking in rubric-based r" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by providing evidence on the same underlying mechanism of "reward hacking" and safety alignment bypass in LLMs, specifically demonstrating how internal model activations (attention heads) can be manipulated to induce misaligned behaviors, which is a core component of the user's interest in detecting and analyzing reward hacking. It also fits criterion (d) as a foundational methodology paper for activation intervention techniques used to probe and steer LLM safe
